buggy_code:
any_positive = np.any(arr > 0, axis=1)
all_positive = np.all(arr > 0, axis=0)
task: Check if any element per row is positive, and if all elements per column are positive
clarification_query: For a 2D array, which axis checks across columns to get one result per row?
corrected_code:
any_positive = np.any(arr > 0, axis=1)
all_positive = np.all(arr > 0, axis=0)
explanation: axis=1 reduces columns → one result per row. axis=0 reduces rows → one result per column. Both usages here are correct.
